description:
You are assisting a marine-biology researcher who stores field-data under 
    /home/user/datasets/coral_reef.  
    The project already contains two files that track releases:

      • /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/VERSION  
        (plain-text file, currently contains the string “1.2.3” followed by a
        single trailing newline)

      • /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/CHANGELOG.md  
        A Markdown changelog whose first few lines are exactly:

            ## [1.2.3] - 2023-01-10
            ### Added
            - Added January 2023 reef imagery.

            ## [1.2.2] - 2022-12-20
            ### Fixed
            - Corrected GPS coordinates.

    The researcher has just integrated a new salinity-measurement data set and
    wants to publish it as version 1.2.4.

    Your job is to perform a very small “semantic-version bump” consisting of
    three results:

    1. Update /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/VERSION so that it **contains only**
       the string

           1.2.4

       followed by a single Unix newline character (LF, 0x0A).  
       No extra spaces or carriage returns are allowed.

    2. Prepend a new release section to /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/CHANGELOG.md.
       After you finish, the first eight non-blank lines of the file **must** be
       exactly (including case, spacing and punctuation):

           ## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15
           ### Added
           - Integrated February 2023 salinity measurements.

           ## [1.2.3] - 2023-01-10
           ### Added
           - Added January 2023 reef imagery.

       Do not modify any of the older sections; simply insert the four new lines
       (plus a blank line) at the top so the historical order is preserved.

    3. Create a simple textual log of the bump at  

           /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/logs/version_bump.log

       The file (which does **not** yet exist) must be created along with its
       parent directory “logs”.  It must contain **exactly one** line, including
       its trailing newline:

           Bumped from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4 on 2023-01-15

       There should be no additional lines or whitespace.

    All paths are fixed and must be used verbatim.  
    The above three artefacts are the only things the automated tests will
    inspect, so pay close attention to spelling, whitespace, and blank lines.

    Hints (do not print them):  
    • You will likely use cat, echo, printf, sed, or similar tools to edit files.  
    • The entire task can be accomplished with only two or three shell commands,
      but any working approach is acceptable.

    When you are finished, verify the files manually if you wish, but remember
    that only the exact contents described above will pass the automated check.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the container
    ---------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/datasets/coral_reef exists and is writable.
    2. /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/VERSION  
       Content (including the final newline):

           1.2.3\n
    3. /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/CHANGELOG.md  
       Content (including blank line between sections):

           ## [1.2.3] - 2023-01-10
           ### Added
           - Added January 2023 reef imagery.

           ## [1.2.2] - 2022-12-20
           ### Fixed
           - Corrected GPS coordinates.

    4. No directory named /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/logs currently exists.

    Required final state for successful evaluation
    -----------------------------------------------
    A. /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/VERSION  
       Exact bytes:

           31 2E 32 2E 34 0A   (ASCII: “1.2.4\n”)

    B. First eight non-blank lines of
       /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/CHANGELOG.md must be exactly:

           ## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15
           ### Added
           - Integrated February 2023 salinity measurements.

           ## [1.2.3] - 2023-01-10
           ### Added
           - Added January 2023 reef imagery.

       The rest of the original content (starting with the blank line that
       separates 1.2.3 from 1.2.2) must remain unchanged.

    C. /home/user/datasets/coral_reef/logs/version_bump.log exists, with parent
       directory created if necessary, permissions 0644 or less restrictive, and
       contains exactly:

           Bumped from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4 on 2023-01-15\n
